questions.text: 7th period of the periodic table contains normal elements:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The period 7 is incomplete so far. It contains only two normal elements Fr87 and Ra88 , ten transition elements and fourteen inner transition elements. The inner transition elements of this period are called Actinides, as they follow 89Ac.The actinides are also shown at the bottom of the periodic table under the Lanthanides. Due to their scarcity, the inner transition elements are also called rare earth elements.

questions.text: Periodic table has been divided into four blocks, which blocks contain highest elements:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Elements in the periodic table can also be classified into four blocks.This classification is based upon the valence orbital of the element involved in chemical bonding. According to this classification, elements of IA and IIA subgroups are called s-block elements because their valence electrons are available in s orbital.The elements of IIIA to VlllA subgroups (except He) areknown as p-block elements as their valence electrons are present in p orbital.Similarly in transition elements, electrons in d-orbital are responsible for their valency hence they are called d-block elements. For Lanthanides and Actinides valence electrons are present in f- orbital hence these elements are called f-block elements. This classification is quite useful in understanding the chemistry of elements and predicting their properties especially the concept of valency or oxidation state.

questions.text: Keeping in view the size of atoms, which order is the correct one:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Mg has less number of shell then Sr so it is smaller in size
Ba and magnsium are in same group . Ba comes after Mg it means it has more shell so it has large atomic size
Lu has less number of shell then Ce so it is smaller in size
Cl has less number of shell then I so it is smaller in size

questions.explanation: Ba and magnsium are in same group . Ba comes after Mg it means it has more shell so it has large atomic sizeAtomic Radius:Atoms are so small that it is impossible to see an atom even with a powerful optical microscope. The size of a single atom therefore cannot be directly measured. However, techniques have been developed which can measure the distance between the centres of two bonded atoms of any element. Half of this distance is considered to be the radius of the atom. In the periodic table, the atomic radius increases from top to bottom within a group due to increase in atomic number . This is because of the addition of an extra shell of electrons in each period. In a period, however, as the atomic number increases from left to right, the atomic radius decreases . This gradual decrease in the radius is due to increase in the positive charge in the nucleus . As the positive nuclear charge increases, the negatively charged electrons in the shells are pulled closer to the nucleus. Thus, the size of the outermost shell becomes gradually smaller. This effect is quite remarkable in the elements of longer periods in which “d” and “f ” subshells are involved. For example, the gradual reduction in the size of Lanthanides is significant and called Lanthanide Contraction.

questions.text: Mosley concluded by the X-ray studies of the elements that physical and chemical properties of elements depend upon:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The atomic number has been established as the fundamental characteristic of elements based on the work on x-ray spectra of elements. According to Moseley's periodic law the physical and chemical properties of elements are the periodic function of their atomic .

questions.text: Which one of the following is not ionic hydride:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is ionic
It is ionic
It is convalent
It is ionic

questions.explanation: The binary compounds of hydrogen with other elements are called hydrides. According to the nature of bonding, hydrides may be broadly classified into three classes: ionic, covalent and intermediate. The elements of group IA and the heavier members of group IIA form ionic hydrides, which contain H- (Hydride) ion.These hydrides are crystalline solid compounds, with high melting and boiling points and which conduct electricity in molten state.

questions.text: The element with highest first ionization energy is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: First ioniztion energy is not the highest of B
First ioniztion energy is not the highest of C
First ioniztion energy is not the highest of C
Nitrogen has highest ionization energy because its outer shell subshell are partially filled

questions.explanation: Generally, smaller the atom, with greater nuclear charge, more strongly the electrons are bound to the nucleus, and hence higher the ionization energy of the atom. By moving from left to right in a period, the outer shell remains the same, while the nuclear charge increases effectively, making the removal of an electron difficult, and hence the value of ionization energy increases. Although the number of electrons also increases in this case, the shielding is not very effective within the same shell. The trend of ionization energies of short periods is shown in Fig. 1.4. The figure also reveals that inert gases have the highest values of ionization energy because, due to complete outermost shell in them, the removal of an electron is extreme.

questions.text: Mark the correct statement:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The cationic size is smaller than normal atomic size
Wrong
The anionic size is larger than atomic size

options.text: Cl- ion and Cl are equal
options.explanation: The anionic size is larger than atomic size

questions.explanation: When a neutral atom loses one or more electrons, it becomes a positive ion. The size of the atom is decreased in this process because of two reasons. First, the removal of one or more electrons from a neutral atom usually results in the loss of the outermost shell. Second, the removal of electrons causes an imbalance in the proton-electron ratio. Due to the greater attraction of the nuclear charge, the remaining electrons of the ion are drawn closer to the nucleus. Thus, a positive ion is always smaller than the neutral atom from which it is derived. The radius of Na is 157 pm, and the radius of Na+ is 95 pm

questions.text: The element with atomic number 9 is closest in chemical properties with element of atomic-number:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: As they belong to the same group, group 7A, they have the same chemical properties. Atomic number 9 is for fluorine, and atomic number 17 is for chlorine.

questions.text: Period six in periodic table contain which “block” elements:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Period 6 contain all the blocks elemnts

questions.explanation: The period 6 is also a long period, which contains thirty-two elements. In this period, there are eight representative elements, ten transition elements, and a new set of fourteen elements called Lanthanides, as they start after 57La. Lanthanides have remarkably similar properties and are usually shown separately at the bottom of the periodic table.

questions.text: Who introduced the zero group:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: He discovered zero group to fit noble gases

questions.explanation: Group VIIIA elements, which are also called zero group elements, usually show zero oxidation state because there is no vacancy in their outermost shells.Mendeleev created the 'zero group' only to fit the noble gases which were discovered towards the end of 19th century.

questions.text: Which element of the following shows max. oxidation state:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Mn has 7 oxidation state

questions.explanation: Transition elements, which are shown in B subgroups of the periodic table, also show the oxidation states equal to their group number as it can be seen for Cu(I), Zn(II), V(V), Cr(VI) and Mn (VII). But due to greater number of valence electrons available in partly filled d-orbitals these elements usually, show more than one oxidation states in their compoundsMn has greater number of oxidation state which is till +7

questions.text: M.Pt is highest for the elements of group:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The melting point is greater for group 4A because of there network structure

questions.explanation: The melting and boiling points of IA and IIA group elements decrease from top to bottom due to the increase in their atomic sizes. The binding forces present between large sized atoms are relatively weaker as compared to those between smaller atoms, For elements of group VIIA, which exist in the form of molecules, the melting and boiling points increase down the group, This is because large molecules exert stronger force of attraction due to their higher polarizabilities.

questions.text: Keeping in view the size of atoms, which orders is the correct one:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: .Ba and magnsium are in same group . Ba comes after Mg it means it has more shell so it has large atomic size

questions.explanation: While Ba and Mg are in the same group, Ba has more shells than Mg, leading to a larger atomic size due to the addition of extra electron shells. This trend follows the periodic trend where atomic radius increases down a group due to the addition of shells, and decreases across a period due to increasing nuclear charge pulling electrons closer.

questions.text: Which statement is correct:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Hydrogen resembles in properties with IA, IVA and VIIA elements
options.explanation: As hydrogen belongs to group 1A and is present in gas state

options.text: Hydrogen resembles in properties with IIIA, IVA and VA elements
Hydrogen resembles in properties with IIA, IVA and VIA elements
Hydrogen resembles in properties with IIA, IIIA and VIIA elements

questions.explanation: Hydrogen's placement at the top of Group IA is due to its similarity in properties with alkali metals, such as having one electron in its outer shell that it can lose to form ions, despite being a nonmetal. Additionally, its resemblance to halogens, such as forming stable diatomic molecules and hydride ions, also justifies its placement at the top of Group VIIA.
